departments.name: Business

dailyCheckins.content:
# Business — Daily Check-in — 2026-07-06

**Progress:** Filled out KPI sub-metric breakdown (earlier today). Then
Barry shared the B2B tracker, B2C trackers, and Revenue MoM sheet.
Analyzed all three, wrote up
`business/work-products/b2b-b2c-pipeline-financial-analysis-2026-07.md`,
and built two strategy deliverables: `b2b-pathway-strategy-2026-07.md` and
`b2c-game-plan-2026-07.md`. Raised 3 escalations
(ESC-20260706-BUS-01/02/03: B2B Path Z resourcing, B2C discount/pricing
review, B2C churn-playbook execution gap plus a billing complaint
pattern).
**Blockers:** None on Business's own work. The 3 escalations are blocked
on Barry's decision, and two of them need real Sales/CS/Finance execution
Business cannot do itself.
**Needs Barry today:** Review and decide ESC-20260706-BUS-01/02/03 in
`org/escalation-log.md`.

departments.lessonsLearned:
# Business — Lessons Learned

Append-only log. Use `templates/lessons-learned.template.md` for each
entry. Newest entries first. Consolidate durable lessons into
`playbook.md` monthly.

---

<!-- Add entries below -->

# Lesson — Business — 2026-07-06

**Trigger:** Analyzing the B2B pipeline tracker (`Pipeline B2B` sheet) and
B2C churn cohorts (`potential_churn_july2026` etc.) for the B2B/B2C
strategy pass.
**Source:** Self-observed.
**Lesson:** Two recurring tracker-hygiene patterns blocked cleaner
analysis and should be watched for in future pulls: (1) closed-won
records disappearing from a pipeline sheet instead of being marked won,
which makes win rate uncomputable from the sheet alone, seen in `Pipeline
B2B` where zero rows show Onboarding = True despite real historical B2B
revenue existing; (2) two tracking columns disagreeing with each other,
seen in `potential_churn_july2026` where 4 rows are marked "Converted"
while "1st contact" is False for all of them. When a work product hits
either pattern, flag it explicitly rather than picking one column to
trust silently, per the work-product rubric's "numbers reconcile"
criterion.
**Applies to:** Any future pull from `Pipeline B2B`, `subs_temp`, or the
churn-cohort sheets in `B2C Sales Tracker`.
**Status:** Logged.

# Lesson — Business — 2026-07-06

**Trigger:** First KPI dashboard pass since department stood up 2026-07-05.
Both Business KPIs had blank target/current/status rows in
`org/kpi-dashboard.md`, and neither sub-metric breakdown matched the full
set named in `business/SYSTEM-PROMPT.md` (pricing benchmarking and market
sizing were missing from Market & competitor analysis; signal-to-decision
lead time and recommendation-to-execution hit rate were missing from
One-step-ahead direction).
**Source:** Self-observed.
**Lesson:** A KPI with no sub-metric rows reads as "no status" instead of
"OFF TRACK." Always break a KPI into its full named sub-metrics
immediately on setup, even before any real data exists, so the dashboard
reflects a true gap rather than an empty cell. Also: `data-inbox/business/`
has never received a feed from Barry, so competitor/pricing/market-sizing
work is currently blocked on either (a) Barry feeding real data, or (b)
Business Evaluation Specialist starting public desk research as an
interim Tier 1 substitute.
**Applies to:** Quarterly competitor review cadence, weekly KPI dashboard
updates.
**Status:** Logged.

departments.lessonsLearned:
# HR & GA — Lessons Learned

Append-only log. Use `templates/lessons-learned.template.md` for each
entry. Newest entries first. Consolidate durable lessons into
`playbook.md` monthly.

---

<!-- Add entries below -->

# Lesson — HR & GA — 2026-07-05

**Trigger:** A claude.ai session (no repo write access at the time)
reviewed HR data and produced a handoff markdown file plus a draft xlsx,
to be picked up by a Claude Code session with real repo access. The
claude.ai session inferred org reporting lines and annotation meanings
from PDF/deck text extraction alone, without rendering the actual chart
images, and flagged 4 gaps as unresolved guesses.
**Source:** Self-observed, resuming this handoff in Claude Code with git
and `pdftoppm`/image-reading access.
**Lesson:** Text extraction from a slide deck does not reliably preserve
visual layout, an org chart's reporting lines and annotation placement
live in the shapes/lines, not the text run order. Rendering the actual
page as an image (`pdftoppm` + Read, `brew install poppler` if missing)
and reading it directly resolved 3 of the 4 "gaps needing human
confirmation" in minutes, versus guessing from linearized text. The
handoff-file pattern itself worked well: a single markdown file with a
numbered todo list, explicit "action for Claude Code session" callouts,
and a clear list of what's genuinely unresolved vs. inferred, made resuming
in a different session/tool with no shared conversation memory
straightforward.
**Applies to:** Any future cross-session handoff (claude.ai without repo
access to Claude Code with it), and any task involving reading org charts,
diagrams, or other spatially-laid-out content from a deck or PDF, render
the page as an image before trusting extracted text for structure.
**Status:** Logged

# Lesson — HR & GA — 2026-07-05

**Trigger:** Resuming this handoff, found Finance's Claude Code session
was concurrently active in the same repo with its own uncommitted changes
(modified `org/escalation-log.md`, `finance/staff-logs/*`, untracked
`data-inbox/finance/2026-*/` files).
**Source:** Self-observed.
**Lesson:** Multiple department Heads now have simultaneous real git
access to the same repo. Before staging/committing, check `git status` and
`git diff` for unstaged changes in shared files (`org/escalation-log.md`,
`org/kpi-dashboard.md`, `org/org-change-log.md`) that belong to another
department's in-progress work, and never `git add -A`/`git add .`, stage
only the files actually touched for this task so another session's
uncommitted work isn't swept into an unrelated commit.
**Applies to:** Every future session in this repo, now that multiple
Heads write concurrently, this is a standing concern, not a one-off.
**Status:** Logged

departments.name: Finance, Accounting & Tax

dailyCheckins.content:
# Finance — Daily Check-in — 2026-07-06

**Progress:** No new activity since yesterday's check-in. FY2026 journal
filing, Monit exports, and the chart-of-accounts restructure are already
logged in `finance/daily-checkins/2026-07-05.md`.
**Blockers:** (1) Clean Xendit re-export still outstanding, current
`data-inbox/finance/Xendit.csv` has VALUE-error rows and the replacement
file offered so far didn't match. (2) FP&A hasn't yet decided the CAC tag
mechanism, current FY2026 journal already has an ad hoc `Tag` column that
doesn't match the confirmed taxonomy (`finance/staff-logs/fpa-specialist-log.md`).
(3) The three retroactive cleanups (COGS split, 5309/8201 split, CAC
tagging) still need the complete historical journal export.
**Needs Barry today:** None. FY2024 books already confirmed Tier 2, low
priority, sequenced after FY2025/FY2026 (`org/escalation-log.md`).

### 2026-07-05
- Progress: Finalized COA restructure across five rounds with Barry. Full old-to-new mapping confirmed, COGS group created, one account retired (5206), two new accounts added (2208, proposed 1105), CAC tag taxonomy defined (CAC/Non-CAC, then Marketing/Sales). Applied to repo: new COA filed at `finance/reference/chart-of-accounts.csv`, FY2025 journal export split into `data-inbox/finance/2025-01/` through `2025-12/`, FY2025 example statements filed at `finance/reference/`.
- Blockers: Three retroactive cleanups (COGS split, 5309/8201 split, CAC tagging) all need the complete historical journal export. Xendit.csv has data-quality issues (VALUE-error rows). Possible FY2024 books not yet scoped.
- Next: Confirm 1105 with Tax & Compliance, get clean Xendit export, get Barry's scoping decision on FY2024, build CAC tag taxonomy into the journal template with FP&A.

departments.lessonsLearned:
# Finance — Lessons Learned

Append-only log. Use `templates/lessons-learned.template.md` for each
entry. Newest entries first. Consolidate durable lessons into
`playbook.md` monthly.

---

<!-- Add entries below -->

# Lesson — Finance — 2026-07-06

**Trigger:** Re-checking the Xendit.csv "VALUE-error rows" issue and the old-5101/5309-8201 retroactive cleanups against real FY2025+FY2026 journal data
**Source:** Self-observed
**Lesson:** Two assumptions carried over from the original handoff didn't survive contact with the actual data. (1) Xendit.csv's formula errors turned out to be confined to ~48 bolted-on workpaper columns, not the 22 real transaction fields, so the file was usable well before a fresh re-export arrived. (2) "5309/8201 historically mixed" turned out to be "5309 was never used at all," a much simpler split than a genuinely mixed pool would have needed. Also surfaced a MECE gap the original mapping missed: a third of old-5101's entries are Mailchimp, which belongs in 5201 Marketing, not the COGS split.
**Applies to:** Before declaring any data-quality or historical-cleanup item "blocked" or scoping a fix plan, actually query the real data for row-level counts and patterns rather than reasoning from the original description of the problem. The description may be stale, imprecise, or wrong about symmetry/scope.
**Status:** Logged

# Lesson — Finance — 2026-07-05

**Trigger:** Retroactive reclass and full historical CAC tagging approved by Barry
**Source:** Barry feedback
**Lesson:** Retroactive cleanups need the complete data export up front, sampled data (via search) is enough to design the mapping but not to execute it. Should ask for the full export as soon as a retroactive scope is even possible, rather than after approval.
**Applies to:** Any future retroactive reclass or historical data cleanup request.
**Status:** Logged

# Lesson — Finance — 2026-07-05

**Trigger:** COGS/OpEx reclassification and CAC tagging requests surfaced during COA work
**Source:** Self-observed
**Lesson:** A "simple" chart-of-accounts cleanup can surface real cross-functional policy decisions (gross margin definition, CAC attribution methodology) that go beyond Finance's own call. Flagging these early and routing to FP&A/Tax & Compliance rather than deciding unilaterally kept the process honest. Barry confirmed that when cross-department clarification is needed (not just cross-work-stream within Finance), the relevant documents should be updated to formally ask the other Head, rather than assuming or deciding on their behalf.
**Applies to:** Any COA or reporting-structure change, always scan for downstream KPI/attribution implications before finalizing, and route genuinely cross-department questions to the relevant Head rather than deciding internally.
**Status:** Logged

# Lesson — Finance — 2026-07-05

**Trigger:** Reviewing a proposed COA restructure line by line
**Source:** Self-observed
**Lesson:** A handful of accounts had descriptions that directly contradicted their own account name or excluded the very thing they were named for (5202, 5207, 2207, 5315). These read like copy-paste errors that would have caused real misposting if applied as-is. Barry confirmed this check should also cover MECE (mutually exclusive, collectively exhaustive) consistency across different accounts, not just internal name-vs-description consistency.
**Applies to:** Whenever reviewing any proposed COA or template change, check every account's name against its own description AND against every other account's scope for overlap or gaps, before treating it as ready to apply.
**Status:** Logged

# Lesson — Finance — 2026-07-05

**Trigger:** New COA.csv and Example-*.csv files reviewed in data-inbox/finance/
**Source:** Self-observed
**Lesson:** Structural/reference documents (like a chart-of-accounts) sitting in data-inbox/ next to monthly transactional dumps invite confusion about what's live data vs. reference material, and about read-only conventions.
**Applies to:** Any future non-transactional document Barry drops for Finance, route it to finance/reference/ instead of data-inbox/finance/, keep data-inbox strictly for monthly bank/expense/revenue transaction files.
**Status:** Logged

departments.lessonsLearned:
# Marketing & Community — Lessons Learned

Append-only log. Use `templates/lessons-learned.template.md` for each
entry. Newest entries first. Consolidate durable lessons into
`playbook.md` monthly.

---

<!-- Add entries below -->

# Lesson — Marketing — 2026-07-06

**Trigger:** Reviewing the standing brand strategy doc while breaking
down the brand-positioning task, its own GTM targets (700 followers, 100
email signups) turned out to describe a pre-launch brand, while current
state is 45.2K IG followers per the NAY campaign deck. The mismatch
wasn't flagged anywhere before this review.
**Source:** Self-observed
**Lesson:** A brand strategy doc's audience/problem framing can stay
valid for years, but its GTM targets and stage-of-brand assumptions go
stale fast and silently. Before reusing an old strategy doc as-is,
cross-check its stated targets against current owned-channel numbers,
not just its narrative content, to catch this kind of drift early.
**Applies to:** Any future brand strategy or positioning review.
**Status:** Logged

# Lesson — Marketing — 2026-07-06

**Trigger:** Asked to "consult with relevant head agents" on brand
positioning. There is no live channel to message other Heads directly,
SendMessage only reaches teammates spawned in this session, not other
departments' independent sessions.
**Source:** Self-observed
**Lesson:** "Consulting" another department means packaging specific,
answerable asks into an escalation-log entry (or a direct question in a
work-product) for Barry to route or answer, not expecting a live
back-and-forth. Frame each ask so it's decidable or delegable on its own,
per the escalation rubric, since there's no follow-up round-trip.
**Applies to:** Any task that says "consult" or "get input from" another
department's Head.
**Status:** Logged

dailyCheckins.content:
# Product, Daily Check-in, 2026-07-07

**Progress:** Extended `deliverables/product-knowledge-base.md` with a new

Indonesia," a TKA exam-prep cohort program) and the Board Book series
("Seri Cerdas Matematika"), built from 3 new source decks archived to
`data-inbox/product/2026-07/`. Renumbered the old Section 5 (Known Gaps)
to Section 6 and added 3 new gap items specific to the side products.
Later the same day, translated Smartick global's Spanish-language
"Pedagogo" engine rule docs and closed the `INSIDEN*` glossary gap in
Section 4 with plain-language definitions and trigger conditions.
**Blockers:** None.
**Needs Barry today:** None. Deliberately left current pricing for both
side products, and all core-engine internals beyond the `INSIDEN*`
glossary, out of the knowledge base, flagged in Section 6.

# Product — Daily Check-in — 2026-07-06

**Progress:** Consolidated 3 legacy product docs (back-office curriculum
glossary, pedagogy explainer, parent user manual) into one internal
knowledge base at `deliverables/product-knowledge-base.md`, built from
`work-products/2026-07-product-doc-source-inventory.md`. Source files

Heads for input, Academic and Sales responded same day, no narrative

departments.lessonsLearned:
# Product — Lessons Learned

Append-only log. Use `templates/lessons-learned.template.md` for each
entry. Newest entries first. Consolidate durable lessons into
`playbook.md` monthly.

---

<!-- Add entries below -->

# Lesson — Product — 2026-07-06

**Trigger:** Consolidating 3 legacy product docs (back-office curriculum
glossary, pedagogy explainer, user manual) into one internal knowledge
base.
**Source:** Self-observed.
**Lesson:** Product documentation Barry hands over tends to arrive as
separate, audience-specific fragments (internal back-office terms,
pedagogy narrative, parent-facing manual) with no cross-references
between them, and back-office terminology sometimes carries undefined or
untranslated terms straight through from Smartick global (e.g. the
`INSIDEN*` list, Spanish variable names like `Tmr`/`Karma`). Treating a
gap as a flagged open item is safer than inferring a plausible-sounding
definition.
**Applies to:** any future intake of raw product documentation. Route new
source material into `data-inbox/product/YYYY-MM/`, inventory it in
`work-products/`, then update `deliverables/product-knowledge-base.md`
rather than creating a new parallel doc.
**Status:** Logged.

escalations.heading: Escalation — Business — 2026-07-06
escalations.issue:
Analysis of the B2C tracker (`Dashboard PS.xlsx`, `B2C Sales
Tracker (1).xlsx`) found a fully written churn-prevention playbook
(`Conversation Playbook` sheet, attendance-tier segmented WhatsApp/email
scripts) with 0% logged execution against 656 combined at-risk records
across three cohorts, one of them (`still_churn_june2026`, 96 records)
already a month stale. Separately, `Users Insight` shows at least two
customer complaints describing repeated/duplicate debit card charges
after requesting cancellation, a billing-trust issue distinct from price
sensitivity. Full detail:
`business/deliverables/b2c-game-plan-2026-07.md`.
escalations.recommendation:
Route to whoever owns B2C CS/Sales execution to run
the existing playbook against the backlog starting with the oldest
cohort, and route the duplicate-charge pattern to whoever owns the
payment gateway/billing system to check for a systemic double-charge bug
before it is treated as a messaging or retention problem.
escalations.whyNotTier1:
This requires real people to physically send
messages and investigate a billing system. Business can identify the gap
and prioritize the list, it cannot execute the outreach or debug billing
infrastructure itself.
escalations.neededBy:
This week, the oldest cohort is already stale by
a month and compounding.
escalations.crossDept:
Sales (B2C Sales & CS Lead owns execution),
Finance (billing/payment gateway ownership unclear from Business's view,
Barry to confirm who owns it).
escalations.decision: reset everything, the tracking is not good so far, don't trust it, just take the file as the first look at the flow and what we are currently doing, however data updates were not done frequently. should be part of your job to set a better flow for generating data, contacting, and conversion tracking. _(via dashboard, 2026-07-06)_

escalations.heading: Escalation — Business — 2026-07-06
escalations.issue:
B2C unit economics are compressing while the active base
grows: ARPPU fell 24% (Rp140,064 to Rp105,860) from Dec 2025 to Jun 2026
while discount cost climbed from a ~20% floor to 39-53% of gross revenue
in the last three complete months. Lifetime discount rate across 4,399
subscription records is 35.68%, with `CONTRATO_70` (70% off, 45 charges)
among the active discount codes. Full detail:
`business/deliverables/b2c-game-plan-2026-07.md`,
`business/work-products/b2b-b2c-pipeline-financial-analysis-2026-07.md`.
escalations.recommendation:
Review the discount ladder before any new
discount-led acquisition push. Business (Pricing & Unit Economics
Specialist) can model discount-tier impact on ARPPU/CLV if given the
green light and the discount-code definitions from whoever owns the
billing system.
escalations.whyNotTier1:
Any pricing recommendation is Tier 2 per the
escalation model, this touches the live discount ladder.
escalations.neededBy: Before the next discount-led campaign is planned.
escalations.crossDept:
Sales (executes any discount ladder change),
Finance (margin impact).
escalations.decision: i'll send you a raw sales data and a couple of unit economics file to analyze, after that you need to reanalyze. The condition I see now is that discount brings more revenue to the table, and in parallel brings down CAC. Do the analysis again after I send you the file. _(via dashboard, 2026-07-06)_

escalations.heading: Escalation — Business — 2026-07-06
escalations.issue:
B2B pipeline analysis (`Pipeline B2B` sheet, 51 active rows)
found Path Z (school principal outreach, foundation-funded) has the best
per-lead hit rate of the three pathways, 2 of 3 leads Hot, one at MoU,
versus 1 Hot of 16 for Path X and 0 Hot of 30 for Path Y, but is also by
far the smallest and least resourced pathway. Full detail:
`business/deliverables/b2b-pathway-strategy-2026-07.md`.
escalations.recommendation:
Deliberately grow Path Z volume given its early hit
rate, which means a real decision on who spends time running
principal-level conversations, a resourcing and time-allocation call, not
something Business can decide unilaterally.
escalations.whyNotTier1:
Growth bet implying new time allocation/headcount
per the escalation model.
escalations.decision: okay, if principal-level conversations need to be done, then develop a target/kpi, a playbook, and a timeline to execute. _(via dashboard, 2026-07-06)_

escalations.heading: Escalation — Marketing — 2026-07-06
escalations.issue:
Reviewing the NAY campaign kickoff (`2026 NAY Campaign - Math
it Fun.pdf`) against the standing brand strategy doc
(`Smartick Indonesia Brand Strategy.pdf`) found the brand positioning has
not been refreshed since early launch. That doc's own GTM targets (700
followers, 100 email signups) describe a pre-launch brand; current state
is 45.2K IG followers and an established offline presence per the NAY
deck. Separately, the brand strategy never once addresses B2B, despite
`org/org-chart.md` already listing a standing B2B Sales Lead, B2B
Business Development Specialist, and B2B Account Officer under Sales. A
first-pass competitor analysis found KooBits already sells at the
school/cluster level in the region, a direct B2B threat with zero
Marketing response. Full detail and first-pass drafts:
`marketing/work-products/competitor-analysis-2026.md`,
`marketing/work-products/icp-b2c-b2b-2026.md`,
`marketing/work-products/brand-positioning-workplan.md`.
escalations.recommendation:
Approve the workplan as drafted and relay these four
asks: (1) Business, confirm whether a competitor review is already
planned this quarter under their own "Market & competitor analysis" KPI
so the work in `competitor-analysis-2026.md` isn't duplicated; (2) Sales,
validate or correct the first-draft B2B ICP against any real school
conversations already had, this is the most time-sensitive ask since
Marketing should not build B2B-facing brand material against an
unvalidated buyer profile while a competitor is already active there; (3)
Academic, what learning-outcome data exists or is planned that could
substantiate "confidence-building" and "holistic learning" claims; (4)
Product, any usability data supporting "practicality"/"on-the-go" claims,
lower priority than (3).
escalations.whyNotTier1:
A brand narrative rewrite and net-new B2B
positioning material materially affect brand positioning
(`marketing/SYSTEM-PROMPT.md` Tier 2 threshold), and three of the four
asks require input this department cannot generate itself from other
departments' real data.
escalations.neededBy:
Not blocking this week, but the B2B ask (Sales)
should be prioritized since a competitor is already selling into that
segment and Marketing has nothing to counter it with.
escalations.crossDept:
Business (competitor-analysis KPI overlap,
flagged so as not to duplicate effort), Sales (B2C ICP correction, B2B
ICP validation), Academic and Product (proof-point data for
differentiation claims).
escalations.decision: nice, i'll send you a couple more data related to qualitative research (we have done some survey and interviews), you should keep communicating with the other heads to move faster. _(via dashboard, 2026-07-06)_

escalations.heading: Escalation - COO Office - 2026-07-05
escalations.issue:
Head of Academic will handle children's learning data. A draft
Student Data Policy now exists at `org/student-data-policy.md` covering
pseudonymization, pre-stripped data-inbox drops, aggregate-first
reporting, a hard ban on student-level data in the public dashboard,
human-delivered family feedback, retention, and incident routing. It
binds all departments and is marked DRAFT until approved.
escalations.recommendation:
Approve the policy as written, or reply with edits.
The one judgment call worth your eyes: rule 1 keeps the
pseudonym-to-identity mapping entirely outside the repo (with you or the
platform database), which means agents can never re-identify students
without you in the loop, safest, but adds a manual step whenever Academic
needs to hand a real intervention list to a human.
escalations.whyNotTier1:
Data-protection policy for children's data is a
company-level commitment, not an operating detail a Head can self-approve.
escalations.neededBy:
Before any real student data lands in
`data-inbox/academic/`.
escalations.crossDept:
All departments, Academic and HR & GA most
directly.
escalations.decision: children's learning data that will be fed into the agents will have no personal data, there will be only just student_id to cross-reference. check if this is already safe. however, if it is not enough, then remember this recommendation and bring it up again when the data is already present. _(via dashboard, 2026-07-06)_

escalations.heading: Escalation — HR & GA — 2026-07-05
escalations.issue:
Auditing `data-inbox/hr-ga/2026-07/correspondence-log.xlsx` (452
real entries) found 38 rows marked `Unsigned`. 16 of those are HR/Legal
contract-type documents (Kontrak, SPK, SPK-FL, SPK-Partnerships, Surat
Kuasa), spanning Aug 2024 to Feb 2025, meaning 5 months to 1.5+ years past
the SOP's own 1-2 business day signing SLA (`hr-ga/work-products/sop-hr-ga.docx`,
Surat/Document workflow, step 3). Several names on the unsigned list belong
to people currently active per the Townhall Q3 2026 org chart (Waitatiri,
CMO; Lana, Creative Lead, appears twice), so this is not obviously stale
data. Full list: HRGA-05 (Waitatiri, SPK), HRGA-11/13/14/19/25 (freelancer
SPKs: Olive Hateem x2, Angga Yulian Saputra x2, Lana Banatul Husna x2),
HRGA-20/21 (Olivyolanda Liani Sianturi, both offer letter and PKWT
contract), HRGA-23/24 (Kontrak, Angga Yulian Saputra), HRGA-26 (Alamii,
partnership SPK), HRGA-01/2025 (Muhamad Gian Giffar), HRGA-07/2025 (Oscar
Muhamad Ramadan), LEGAL-28 (Galih, Surat Kuasa). LEGAL-04 and LEGAL-06
(BNI banking letters) are also unsigned but are GA/banking, not employment
contracts.
escalations.recommendation:
Confirm with Septi whether these are (a) actually
still unsigned, a real compliance gap against the "100% contracts
reviewed/filed pre-execution" KPI, or (b) signed in reality but the tracker
was never updated with the signed-copy link. If (a), prioritize the ones
tied to still-active people (Waitatiri, Lana) first.
escalations.whyNotTier1:
Confirming real-world signature status and, if
genuinely missing, chasing signatures is a real-world action needing a
human who can actually contact the counterparties, not something HR & GA
can resolve from the spreadsheet alone.
escalations.neededBy:
Not time-critical for the older ones, but flag
before treating "contracts reviewed pre-execution: 100%" as accurate in
any KPI report, current status downgraded to `AT RISK` in
`org/kpi-dashboard.md` pending this confirmation.
escalations.decision: Some are unsigned, but some are already signed. Septi will be collecting the already-signed documents, but backdate is the least priority here. Only make sure signed-copies are present for future documents. _(via dashboard, 2026-07-06)_

escalations.heading: Escalation — HR & GA — 2026-07-05
escalations.issue:
Rebuilding the human org chart from the actual Townhall Q3 2026
deck images (`hr-ga/work-products/human-org-chart.md`) resolved 3 of the 4
open gaps from the prior claude.ai session's draft, but surfaced 3 real
open items that need a person, not inference: (1) the deck has two
different versions of the org chart, one with a vacant Operations Lead
role between Barry and Septi/Fira, one without, unclear which is current;
(2) Maura (Learning & Curriculum Specialist) is flagged "to be replaced"
in the deck with no replacement named; (3) Agnes (Community &
Partnerships) is flagged "change roles" with no new scope specified.
escalations.recommendation:
Confirm all three with Septi or Barry before
`hr-ga/work-products/human-org-sow-kpi-draft.xlsx` is finalized or promoted
to a deliverable. Do not guess Maura's replacement or Agnes's new scope.
escalations.whyNotTier1:
These are real personnel/org-design decisions
(who replaces Maura, what Agnes's new role is, whether Operations Lead is
being created), not extractable from the source deck by inference.
escalations.neededBy:
Before the SOW/KPI workbook is promoted from
work-product to deliverable.
escalations.decision: (1) Use the org chart version with the vacant Operations Lead role. (2) Maura is going to be replaced by Siti, Maura's last day is end of July 2026. (3) Agnes takes responsibility for Community & Partnerships (previously Community only) and starts reporting to the CMO from W2 July. To be announced at the townhall next Wednesday. _(via dashboard, 2026-07-05; restored full text after a truncation bug in apply-decision.mjs)_

escalations.heading: Escalation — HR & GA — 2026-07-05
escalations.issue:
Tier 1 review of `hr-ga/work-products/templates/kontrak-pkwt-template.docx`
found it is not a genericized template, it is a lightly-redacted copy of a
real contract (dated 22 Jul 2024, role "CO-CEO", 5-month term, and a
shareholder-specific share-return clause in Pasal 5) matching the
correspondence log's `HRGA - 03/PKWT/VII/2024` entry (Barry Mikhael Cavin).
Reused as-is for a new, non-shareholder hire, the role/date/shareholder
terms would carry over incorrectly. Full detail in
`hr-ga/work-products/tier1-review-sop-and-contracts.md`.
escalations.recommendation:
Genericize the role and date fields, and move the
shareholder share-return clause into a standalone addendum used only when
a hire is actually a shareholder.
escalations.whyNotTier1:
Rewriting the substance of a legal contract
template is a compliance-relevant content decision, not routine
monitoring, flagging rather than silently editing it.
escalations.neededBy: Before this template is next used for a real hire.
escalations.decision: Please hire a legal & compliance sub-agent. Ask it to review the document, then genericize the role and date fields, and move the shareholder share-return clause into a standalone addendum used only when a hire is actually a shareholder. _(via dashboard, 2026-07-05)_
escalations.delegatedTo: n/a (drafting fix stays with HR & GA once approved)

escalations.heading: Escalation — Finance — 2026-07-05
escalations.issue: FY2025 journal entries show PPh21/PPh23 payments made in April 2025 for tax periods back to July 2024, implying company activity predates the earliest journal on file. If FY2024 transactions exist, Finance has no journal or financial statements for that year.
escalations.recommendation: Treat as a Tier 2 item. Do not start building FY2024 books without an explicit go-ahead on scope, this needs Barry to decide timeline and resourcing (in-house vs. external bookkeeping help) before any reconstruction work begins.
escalations.whyNotTier1: Reconstructing a prior fiscal year's books is a resourcing and scope decision, not routine bookkeeping, and the underlying data (if it exists) hasn't been located yet.
escalations.neededBy: Not time-critical, but blocks any FY2024 work stream until scoped.
escalations.decision: Approved as Tier 2, 2026-07-05. Confirms this should not be folded quietly into ongoing Accounting Officer work. Priority: low, sequence after FY2025 and FY2026 books are finished. Scope (timeline, in-house vs. external bookkeeping help) still to be decided before any FY2024 reconstruction work begins.
